title: Accurate Islamic Prayer Times for Your Location: Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib and Isha
excerpt: Get daily updated, location-precise Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib and Isha prayer times — with full support for all major calculation methods. Understanding why prayer times differ between apps and which method is right for you.
author: QuranBookk
content:
The Quran does not simply say to pray — it specifies that prayer is bound to time:

<AyahBlock
  arabic="إِنَّ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَٰبًا مَّوْقُوتًا"
  translation="Indeed, prayer has been decreed upon the believers a decree of specified times."
  reference="Surah An-Nisa 4:103"
/>

The word used is *mawqutan* — time-bound, scheduled, fixed. Praying outside the appointed window without a valid reason is treated as a serious matter by scholars of all madhabs. The *time* of prayer is not incidental to the prayer — it is part of the obligation itself.

This means that knowing precisely when each prayer begins is not a convenience. It is an Islamic requirement.

## The Five Prayer Times and How They Are Defined

### Fajr — The Dawn Prayer
Fajr begins at **true astronomical dawn** — when the first light appears as a white horizontal stripe along the horizon, not the reddish glow that precedes it (which is the false dawn). This is typically 60–90 minutes before sunrise, though it varies significantly by season and latitude. Fajr ends at sunrise. During Ramadan, Fajr is also the time of Imsak — the moment the fast begins — making its precise calculation especially critical.

### Dhuhr — The Midday Prayer
Dhuhr enters when the sun crosses its zenith (highest point in the sky) and begins its descent. This is **solar noon** — not 12:00 PM on the clock, but the astronomical midpoint of the day. Solar noon can fall anywhere from 11:40 AM to 1:10 PM depending on your longitude within your time zone and the time of year. Dhuhr ends when an object's shadow equals its own length (the entry of Asr by the majority view).

### Asr — The Afternoon Prayer
Asr is the most discussed prayer time in classical Islamic law because the two major schools calculate its entry differently:
- **Majority position (Shafi'i, Maliki, Hanbali)**: Asr begins when an object's shadow equals its own length plus the length of the shadow at Dhuhr.
- **Hanafi position**: Asr begins when an object's shadow equals *twice* its own length plus the Dhuhr shadow.

This difference can result in a 45–75 minute gap between the two Asr times, depending on season and latitude. The [QuranBookk Prayer Times](/prayer-times/) tool allows you to select your madhab preference so the Asr time shown is correct for your school.

### Maghrib — The Sunset Prayer
Maghrib begins at the moment of **astronomical sunset** — when the upper edge of the sun's disc disappears below the horizon. According to the majority of scholars, it should be prayed promptly after sunset without delay. Maghrib ends at the disappearance of the red twilight (the entry of Isha).

### Isha — The Night Prayer
Isha begins when the **red twilight** disappears from the western sky — typically 60–90 minutes after Maghrib. Isha ends at solar midnight (halfway between Maghrib and Fajr) according to most scholars, though some permit it until true Fajr as a necessity.

## Why Different Apps Show Different Prayer Times

If you have ever compared two prayer time apps and found different times for the same location, you have encountered the **calculation method difference**. The primary variation is in how Fajr and Isha are defined — each uses an angle below the horizon that differs by organization:

| Organization | Fajr Angle | Isha Angle | Primary Region |
|---|---|---|---|
| Muslim World League (MWL) | 18° | 17° | Europe, Far East, Americas |
| Islamic Society of North America (ISNA) | 15° | 15° | USA, Canada |
| Egyptian General Authority | 19.5° | 17.5° | Egypt, many Arab countries |
| Umm al-Qura University | 18.5° | 90 min after Maghrib | Saudi Arabia |
| University of Islamic Sciences, Karachi | 18° | 18° | Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, Afghanistan |

A 3° difference in the Fajr angle can shift the Fajr time by 15–20 minutes — which is significant for someone managing Suhoor timing during Ramadan.

<Callout type="tip">
To choose the right calculation method: If you are in the USA or Canada, ISNA is the standard recommendation. For South Asia (Pakistan, India, Bangladesh), the Karachi method is standard. For Saudi Arabia and Gulf countries, Umm al-Qura is standard. When in doubt, ask your local mosque which method they follow — and then set your tool to match.
</Callout>

## The High-Latitude Problem

Muslims in northern latitudes — Scotland, Scandinavia, Canada above 50°N, Alaska — face a unique challenge. During summer months, the sun barely sets or does not set at all. In some Scandinavian cities, the sky never becomes fully dark between June and August, meaning the standard definition of Isha (red twilight disappearing) is technically never met.

Scholars have issued fatwas for these regions recommending one of two approaches:
1. **Nearest city method** — follow the prayer times of the nearest city where normal twilight patterns occur
2. **Fixed proportional hours** — assign Fajr and Isha based on fixed proportions of the night

The [QuranBookk Prayer Times](/prayer-times/) tool applies appropriate high-latitude adjustments automatically based on your location.

## The Sunnah of Each Prayer Time

The Prophet ﷺ gave specific guidance on *when within the time window* to pray each prayer:

<HadithBlock
  text="The best deed is prayer at its proper time."
  narrator="Ibn Masud (رضي الله عنه)"
  source="Sahih al-Bukhari 527"
/>

Specific sunnahs by prayer:
- **Fajr**: Pray in its early time — the Prophet ﷺ prayed Fajr while it was still dim (*ghalas*), not waiting for full light
- **Dhuhr**: In summer, the Prophet ﷺ would sometimes delay Dhuhr slightly (*ibrad*) until the worst of the midday heat had passed
- **Asr**: Do not delay until the sun yellows — the Prophet ﷺ warned against this
- **Maghrib**: Pray immediately — do not delay Maghrib even for breaking fast
- **Isha**: Can be delayed until the first third of the night, which is recommended if it causes no hardship

## The Prayer That Is Prayed at Its Right Time

The scholars unanimously agree: a prayer performed at its appointed time, even if simple and brief, is superior to a lengthened prayer performed late without excuse. Knowing precisely when the time begins is the foundation of every valid Salah.
